check.if: any alternative needs web research AND INTERACTIVE
check.ask: Name the alternative(s) needing external/web research. Ask the user to approve dispatching web-search-researcher, recommended option first. If declined, mark those alternatives' cells as "not researched — web declined" and note the deferral.

check.if: any alternative needs web research AND AUTONOMOUS
check.action:
If web_search_approved is true, keep the external research. If false or absent, emit to stdout and stop:
CLARIFICATION_NEEDED: Comparing {alternatives} requires web research, but web_search_approved was not set. Re-dispatch with web_search_approved=true, or limit to internal alternatives.

substep.title: Dispatch One Track per Alternative
substep.action: Dispatch one focused research question per alternative, all in a SINGLE parallel message via the Task tool. Each prompt names the alternative, the criteria to assess it on, and the expected output shape (per-criterion findings with citations) — per child-subagent-dispatch prompt-anatomy. The prompt carries the alternative and the criteria alone, never which alternative is hoped to win. Route external alternatives to web-search-researcher, internal approaches to the codebase children.

substep.title: Validate Returns and Sharpen-Redispatch
substep.action: Redispatch any vague or partial return with a sharper prompt before building the matrix — a track that did not cover all criteria leaves holes in the grid. A criterion that genuinely cannot be determined for an alternative becomes a "could not determine" cell, which is honest; an evasive return that should have an answer gets re-dispatched.
